notes: This is a high-convergence meeting driven primarily by Sam and Francisco co-presenting the company's strategic vision and engineering philosophy to the full team for the first time. The key convergence dynamic is information asymmetry being resolved: TJ raises a direct runway concern, Sam addresses it with concrete deal ladder details, and TJ explicitly signals resolution ('I appreciate the clarification, so at least I now see a ladder'). Yumi asks about product scope, triggering an extended strategic disclosure that multiple participants build on. Francisco's agent-first manifesto presentation generates engagement rather than resistance. The meeting converges on shared understanding of strategy, commercial path, and engineering philosophy, even though the manifesto's operational implications remain aspirational. | Caveats: This is primarily a founder-led information session, not a negotiation or decision meeting. Convergence is easier when the structure is presentational rather than deliberative.; The manifesto is presented as aspirational. Agreement with the aspiration does not mean agreement with the operational implications, which are deferred.; Yumi's design concern ('designing experience sometimes takes time') is validated but not resolved. This is a latent divergence point that will resurface.; The Sam-Francisco co-narration is very high LSM but may reflect co-founder alignment rather than team-wide convergence. The rest of the team is mostly receiving, not co-constructing.; TJ's runway question was the only genuine stress test. Its clean resolution inflates the overall convergence score. A meeting with more distributed pushback might score lower.
